transcript.text: You too.
How's it going?
This meeting is being recorded.
Pretty well.
It's 80 degrees today in Houston, so we got a tease of winter, and now we're back to a little bit warmer temperatures.
How's the city with the new mayor?
Pretty electric, I'm sure.
I wasn't in, no, he went into the city quickly yesterday.
But yeah, I'm up in Westchester, so hoping just, eh, it doesn't really bother me.
It doesn't affect me.
Makes a lot of sense.
Let's see what happens in subways.
If the trains and subways run on time better, then I'll be happy.
But until then, whatever.
There you go.
There you go.
Are you from the city originally, or how long have you been in New York?
I was in the city for about 15 years.
I went there for college and stayed for a while, and then we've been up in Westchester for the last five.
Okay, nice.
Yeah, I spent one year right before COVID in East Williamsburg, and I was convinced I was going to be...

One of those people who moves to New York and stays for a while, but, you know, the pandemic had other plans, and then I met my fiance when I came back to Texas, where, you know, Texas was still pretending COVID wasn't real, so.
Best city in the world, I think, New York, no doubt about it.
Yeah, sorry, just taking, writing something quickly.
Yeah, I love New York, like, we were working from home for a while, so I wouldn't say I missed it, but now that I go in a couple times a week, it's like, it's fine.
I hate the traffic, but overall, it's just a fun city still.
Definitely.
Well, excited to chat today.
you know, enjoyed connecting with you and Gary last week with Jason, and, you know, there's.
And I gave you access for the stuff you need, right?
Did I give you guys the SEO, the search console access, or no?
I'm trying to, this week's been nuts.
Oh, yeah, not yet, but.
You're not behind schedule or anything there.
I wanted to just make sure that we answer all of your questions first.
You feel good about what the partnership would look like.
I know we still need to talk friends and family pricing, but I didn't want to get ahead of myself.
I wanted to make sure you have all your questions answered about the way we work and what the partnership would entail before we're thinking about the agreement itself.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Okay, great.
so overall, I mean, I really enjoyed meeting with you guys.
So I've met with like five different SEO companies.
I'd say you guys are in the top two, and I'm trying to figure out the differentiation between you and the other one overall.
But I think the first meeting went really well.
I think I'm trying to get like some basic elements of strategy.

You know, with this kind of stuff, it is, I don't want to say it's a complete crapshoot, but part of it is just like there's so much trust that goes into it because you don't see results for three plus months.
We'll see
Yeah, I mean, I was checking you guys' SEMrush, and it's the beginning of the journey, right?
Like, we'd have to build that foundation content for you.
Like, I literally just got off a call with another one, and they're like, so what keywords should we go after?
I'm like, I have no idea, right?
Like, I can come up with keywords that will probably drive traffic, but I don't know if that's going to be quality traffic.
So that's what we really need, like, a strategic partner on.
So there's part of it, which is, like, the strategy as we go from zero, and the other is, like, can you implement with scale quickly?
And I, like, I really like the approach that you all pitched when we chatted.
But, yeah, I mean, what has been your experience of just, like, we have no baseline.
The baseline is, like, zero.
Yeah, we've done that a couple times.
I think Tiro is, like, good case study to look at, and also Betagrid, which I believe Gary mentioned.
Yeah.
Both...
I think like either Seed or Series A, built their entire blog out once they just created the section of the site.
And like the key is being able to get to scale.
And sometimes when we work with technical clients, there's like too much concern about every single article that we're writing being like this thought leadership piece, right?
Like being something that the CEO is going to sign off on for every draft.
And it's not that we can't hit that quality.
It's just more so that's not the best way to drive results at the stage that the domain is at, right?
Like the stage the domain is at, we need to create all this educational content.
We need to target like longer tail keywords.
We need to do definitions, listicles, comparisons.
If we can do that at scale, like we can achieve great results together, it'll still take longer than a company that's got, you know, the domain presence.
And the quantity of indexed pages.
But I think, like, the mismatch is when they're expecting us to come in and write content that their developers would write as the first piece of the engagement.
We won't get to, like, scale if we go about the process that way.
Yeah.
When you talk about getting a scale, like, give me your, starting from zero, what's your just, you know, I'm just looking for directional, not for specifics, but, like, how many keywords?
Should we go after initially how, like, content, call it buckets of categories or whatever, categories that we're going after?
And about how much content do you guys typically push out on a regular basis or on a monthly basis?
We would try to do five articles per week.
The only limitation would be, like, the review process to get the drafts published.
In terms of the number of keywords that we would target, I could just show you examples from what the...
Team has done on the delivery side since I'm more pre-sales, but always trying to learn more there.
The idea is that we're looking at all of the different eligible keywords based on the competitors that you tell us to focus on and the topics, right, where you want to have authority.
And so we would look at like the SEO analysis and then we would also dig into where are the topic clusters where we want to create content assignments.
And then from there, it's like a matter of prioritizing those topics with you and connecting the topics to like the keyword volume and the search opportunity.
So that's like high level.
We can go deeper if you want.
I just don't know like the number of keywords.
I know it's exhaustive, right?
Like we're looking at everything that could be connected to your space and that your competitors are ranking for.
But that's going to be different from every single client.
Right.
Yeah.
What do you I forget if we talked about this last time, but.
Another company was talking about it pretty heavily, but what do you guys do around backlinking and all of that?
How much time do you spend on that stuff?
Yeah, the reality is that we haven't been doing that for clients because it's not something that we can really scale internally.
And a lot of times it can be more of like a money game.
And if you're just creating great content and then thinking about ways to target outreach around the content you've created, that can, you know, address the need.
But if you needed that, I would say, you know, maybe one of the other partners is a better fit.
I don't know if we need I've been told that it's a necessity or used to be a necessity for SEO.
I don't know how much it is now for AI results and where SEO is going.
Yeah, I think it can speed up the process and our team can definitely collaborate with you around.
How do you want to go about it?
We just wouldn't execute that on your behalf unless it was something that we carve out as like a custom addition to the agreement.
But you've had, I assume, good success without it.
Yes, definitely.
I mean, take a look at like the traffic for TRO.com.
Take a look at the traffic for Datagrid.com in terms of some of the proof points, because I know that was important.
Like, discern, 3x their organic traffic with us.
They are ranking one for 346 non-branded keywords that they weren't ranking for before they started with GrowthX.
Exec.com has like 46x their LLM referral traffic in six months with us, going from 16 to 700 sessions.
The focus is definitely, right, like doing what the backlinking strategy would be focused on.
But instead of, right, like paying for everything, it's really just like creating that content engine in-house.
Yeah.
What, how far downstream do you guys look at metrics?
Other than, like, do you go beyond visitation?
Are you looking at quality of traffic or, like, lead volume or conversion rates?
Yeah, definitely.
If we can hook into any sort of, like, existing attribution that you're doing, it would be easy for us to track that granularly.
We'll get access to your Google Analytics, to your Google Search Console.
We'll set up this Looker dashboard.
I've looked into some of those dashboards we've created, and it's, like, the engagement analytics.
I'm not sure if, I haven't seen anything, like, around the quality of the traffic itself, because I'm just not sure how we would differentiate there.
More about if they, the leads convert at a higher rate versus other things.
Yeah, absolutely.
Yeah, looking at the conversion rate, connecting...

Not to the content we produce, just not being able to say, here are all the software developers, right, that have accessed this article versus, you know, our procurement folks versus, like, marketers, for example.
Yeah.
One question for you, just real quick.
Who are, like, the top three competitors that we would look at the opportunity against?
Because sometimes we do that just ahead of the partnership being finalized to, you know, make sure we can give you guys more insight, right, into, like, what the opportunity is.
How many keywords can we go after?
Like, are specific competitors in this?
Yeah.
Yeah, who you'd want to show up in front of, who people are choosing instead of zip.
Yeah.
So that's a couple, right?
We don't really have a direct competitor at the moment.
We're one of the first breaking through in this.
I'll mention a couple of things as far as, like, the keywords to...
One is our partners.
We don't compete with them, but Jamf, J-A-M-F, is an interesting one.
You can buy Jamf through Zip Security.
We partner with them.
When somebody searches for Jamf, I want Zip Security to show up as the easier way to implement Jamf.
Same thing for Intune, for Okta, for CrowdStrike.
We should be showing up on all of their keywords as, like, we should have articles about, like, hey, these are the benefits of using Zip Security to launch these systems or to manage these systems.

So they're not a competitor, but, like, the competition is a company doing it themselves through their own IT team, you know, DIY of their own, like, we're going to provision all five of these things, whereas you just go with Zip and you buy them through the Zip system.
That makes a lot of sense.
I think, to your point, you know, it's the companies that are showing up in search that you would want to show up in as well or in front of.
So that makes
It's a lot of sense with Jamf, Intune, Okta, CrowdStrike.
The other thing are the MSPs, so the managed security providers, MSSPs as well, whatever, they're solution providers.

Yeah, that makes sense.
That's who the department of zero and one hire, basically.
Yeah, don't outsource something you should be able to control yourself.
Yep, and just thinking about direct competitors, maybe it's more of the mid-market segment, and I know you guys aren't as focused on mid-market enterprise right now.
So we're going after 10 to 250 people.
Yeah.
Is there a direct competitor that would be good to look at?
Literally none.
Okay.
Nothing that comes up.
It's basically like outsourcing to one of those MSPs, or they're just like, we're just going to try doing this ourselves.
Or this is not a need today is kind of the other thing.
So, yeah.
But we have enough that have the need.
We just have to reach them.

Yeah.
So let's do that.
You

So instead of going to two different companies here, same thing with Okta, same thing with CrowdStrike.

CrowdStrike and all of them, like Jamf, they actually like us.

Here, you just launch it directly, and here, it's no problem.
Okay, where, like, the MSP would help set up those connections to those individual providers?

They're more, like, advising.
Hmm.
Okay.
Yeah, they will manage all of it, but, like, you get rid of the MSP, and all of a sudden, like, things go to .

It's more around control around all that.
So, yes, there is strategy.
So you can actually go in here and see, like, what do I need to do to be SOC2 compliant or HIPAA compliant?
And it tells you how far you're along and what else you need.
So you need to launch multi-factor authentication.
So you click a button and send your whole team an email to launch it.
Okay.
Okay, nice.
And it tracks how compliant you are as a company and lets you check the box.
Right, right.
Yeah, it's interesting, like, sweet spot because it's, like, you know, there's, like, Drata that simplifies, like, compliance with SOC2 and other different, other certifications.
And then there's, like, the Jamf and Intune that's for, like, the device onboarding and management.
But then you're connected, right, to, like, all of these different things that an IT org is trying to do.
But there's, like, no other company that's doing that.
Yeah, kind of the ultimate
All in one view into everything.
The command center?
Yeah.
Okay.
I think I have a good idea of like what we would look at in terms of the opportunity.
I think you have a good idea of like the content that we would create, right?
Like what we would focus on to start and then what we could scale to more like programmatic types of use cases.
What other questions do you have for me just to, I guess, help you think through like if we're the right fit, if, you know, one of these other agencies you're talking to.
It's a better fit.
Let me see.
I'm just going through some notes I had to see where there's differentiation.
Yeah, we would create five publish ready pieces editorial content per week.
We could swap that out for programmatic SEO.
And we just want to think about.
Yeah.
Like, what's the right play?
We would stage and publish in the CMS, and it's usually best for us to have, like, one or two points of contact so that the review process isn't a bottleneck.
And then we're going to be delivering you guys, like, all the LLM monitoring and visibility.

Um, we've got, like, a platform where all that is surface, citations where you're showing up, prompts, you know, you're tracking how you're progressing in terms of being in the answer versus competitors or versus these other firms like Jamf.
Um, and yeah, I mean, we're basically making our goal to drive higher intent traffic, see it all the way through conversion.
And if we're not doing that, you know, you're not going to keep working with us.
So that's the vision.
So other than, like, a human review on our end, what are you all doing to make sure that the content quality is, uh, above a certain bar and that it's, uh, it's accurate?
Because accuracy is pretty...
What's to our audience?
Yeah, would say like our team is constantly refining those artifacts that we create at the beginning of the partnership.
So that's one piece in terms of quality.
The other piece is that like we're reading every single word and making manual changes.
We're not just like accepting the output.
In terms of fact checking, I can actually show you.
don't think I showed you this last time.
And in our workflows, we create this where there's a chain of thought and agentic workflows as well so that it'll reinforce until it hits a certain quality threshold.
We also like store any of the research that we do in a knowledge graph so that we can pull it in semantically the next time we're creating a new assignment just based on the topic.
And this fact checking step here, you know, what's great about this process is that if you look at the research, so, you know, the research, we're leveraging all these information.
It puts around who is this concept for?
What is the direction from our managing editor?
You know, what are we essentially trying to capture in terms of, like, the brief for this assignment?
And then we're able to point the research to a specific audience.
We can define, like, a research objective and success criteria.
We can focus it on certain source requirements or connect to that knowledge base.

And the idea is that, like, after we've done this process of doing deep research, doing it in a loop so that we can address gaps that come up, we're going to, you know, continue to basically make sure that we're validating it against writing guidelines that you have that we've created as an artifact.

And then with the draft that we've created, you know, from this research that's been performed in a loop, we're actually going in and taking the entire article and then extracting passages and doing more deep research so that if there is anything that we wrote...
That isn't up to, you know, a minus two to two, like confidence interval threshold will actually suggest a rewrite that the managing editor can then come in and accept or alter.
And so there's a good example in here.
Let me see if there's, there's one right there, but yeah, I want to show you this one.
Okay.
So like generate flame graphs with Netflix, 99 Hertz, safe sampling approach for low overhead.
That basically is going in and, you know, getting rationale for why we shouldn't leverage that and providing more of the nuance of, you know, here were the experts that referenced this sampling rate.
The similar example here was we basically had a statement that connected studies to reporting like a certain percentage of maintainability gains, code duplication.
And then, you know, the rationale here is like, there's not a, there's not a widespread.
Spread acceptance that there's exactly this amount of maintainability gains, reduction in code duplications.
While they're widely observed and discussed, the specific percentages are not widely accepted or cited.
And so you think about the GEO impact, this is all connected to, is the content credible?
Is it comprehensive?
Is it current?

And if you're blindly throwing out percentages or you're blindly, you want to be able to have data in the content that you're creating, but if you're doing so without the nuance, that's when you can get penalized for just putting out content that's not fact-checked or not reliable.

I think, you know, I'll stop monologuing, but this is a good look, a good look at like, you know, there's this agentic research, but there's also the fact-checking and then the artifacts are going to inform everything that we create.
We'll do.
That against custom writing guidelines, and those can be direct, like, don't use MDashes only, you know, site sources in this way, whatever it might be.

Well, taking one quick step back before we even get to, like, content building, how do you guys work with us as far as making sure that, like, the pages are templated properly, optimized properly for SEO and Geo?
And then are you also doing the actual, like, content upload to the CMS?

Yeah, so we would work with you guys just to make sure that, like, the templates that you've set up, especially in the blog, are optimized for SEO and Geo, and we'll do all the staging and publishing.
So in terms of, like, how the content is structured and formatted, our team will follow best practices there.
And in terms of, like, all the meta tagging and the SEO details, like, our team would handle that for each piece that we publish.
The thing that we wouldn't be doing is we wouldn't be doing, like, custom web development.
We be doing it.
We We
Unless that was specifically called out in the SOW.
We have a web dev team.
just want to make sure that, like, you tell them what to implement to make it.
Yeah, definitely.
The only thing they need to make sure is implemented is a blog template that we can go in and leverage for the content that we're creating.
Cool.
All right.
Cool.
So, yeah, at this point, I'm evaluating between you guys and probably the one, maybe two others.
But hopefully we'll have a better sense of what we're going to do by mid to late next week.
That sounds great.
And I'll send over our SOW so that you've got it.
And I talked to Marcel just because, like, Jason knows Gary from a lot of the events that we've hosted.
And, you know, the 30K, 15K pricing, we could definitely look at discounting that for you guys.
It would be like 25 and 12.
Since, like, 30, 15K is the lowest we've been charging for new clients.
But we can also, like, be flat.
Flexible on the payment terms for phase two, if it is helpful for you guys.
Nice.
Okay.
That's very helpful.
Yeah, that puts it a little bit more in line with some of the others that I'm seeing.
Perfect.
No, that sounds good.
And who would we work from in terms of your team?
Is there a direct point of contact that would review the drafts?
There will be.
I'm trying to hire a content marketer now, but assume that for the initial part, it might just be working through me.
Yeah.
But hopefully we have somebody before the end of the year.

think ideally that person's there so that you're not the sole point of contact for everything unless you want to be and you just like want to get started as quick as possible.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Finalize Zipsec's evaluation of GrowthX for SEO services.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1462.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Strategic Focus:** Zipsec needs to rank for keywords of partners (Jamf, Okta) and preempt MSPs, as it has no direct competitors.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=618.0)
  - [**Content Engine:** GrowthX will publish 5 educational articles/week to build domain authority from a zero baseline, a strategy proven with clients like Tiro and Betagrid.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=222.0)
  - [**Fact-Checking:** A proprietary agentic workflow ensures accuracy by validating every passage against a confidence threshold, preventing SEO penalties for unreliable content.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1126.0)
  - [**Pricing:** A "friends and family" discount was offered: $25k/mo (Phase 1) and $12k/mo (Phase 2), down from $30k/$15k.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1474.0)

## Topics

### Zipsec's SEO Challenge & Strategic Goals

  - [**Challenge:** Zipsec has a zero SEO baseline and needs a strategic partner to build a foundation for long-term growth.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=164.0)
  - [**Strategic Goals:**](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=618.0)
      - [**Partner Keywords:** Rank for terms like "Jamf," "Intune," and "Okta" by positioning Zipsec as the easier, faster way to implement these tools.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=656.0)
      - [**MSP Preemption:** Position Zipsec as a superior alternative to Managed Security Providers (MSPs).](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=724.0)
  - [**Target Audience:** Small to mid-market companies (10–250 employees).](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=768.0)

### GrowthX's Proposed Solution

  - [**Content Engine:** Publish 5 educational articles/week (definitions, listicles, comparisons) to build domain authority.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=247.0)
  - [**Backlinking:** Not a core service. GrowthX's success (e.g., Tiro, Betagrid) relies on a high-volume content engine, not paid links.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [**Fact-Checking Process:** A proprietary agentic workflow ensures accuracy and credibility.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1126.0)
      - [**Mechanism:** The system extracts passages from drafts and validates them against a confidence threshold.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1243.0)
      - [**Outcome:** Flags nuanced statements (e.g., specific percentages) as potentially unreliable, prompting a human editor to rewrite for accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1243.0)
  - [**Technical SEO:** GrowthX will provide recommendations for SEO-optimized blog templates and will handle all content staging, publishing, and meta-tagging.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1389.0)

### Logistics & Pricing

  - [**Reporting:** A Looker dashboard will track traffic, engagement, and LLM visibility (citations, prompts).](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=548.0)
  - [**Attribution:** GrowthX can hook into Zipsec's existing attribution to track downstream metrics like conversion rates.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=548.0)
  - [**Review Process:** A single point of contact is needed to prevent content review from becoming a bottleneck.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)
      - [**Initial:** Dave Katz.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1513.0)
      - [**Future:** A new Content Marketing Engineer (hiring in progress).](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1513.0)
  - [**Pricing:** A "friends and family" discount was offered.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1474.0)
      - [**Phase 1:** $25k/mo (down from $30k).](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1474.0)
      - [**Phase 2:** $12k/mo (down from $15k).](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1474.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler Pavlas:** Send the Statement of Work (SOW) with discounted pricing.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1462.0)
  - [**Dave Katz:** Make a final decision on an SEO partner by mid-to-late next week.](https://fathom.video/share/e1W_zjL3A_tWfj_wLT3TfYCE7jCizWzs?tab=summary&timestamp=1462.0)
